translation.literal_translation: If it be said, 'it speaks thus,' no; for it is the Pragna, because of the context. (5)
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.5: even if one claims the cited language supports pradhana, that reading is rejected; contextual continuity establishes Pragna (the conscious supreme self) as the intended referent.
translation.interpretive_note: Prakarana is decisive over isolated lexical overlap with Sankhya terms.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Literal preserves compact sutra style and may need coordination with your preferred idiom for cet references.
Technical line depends on continuity with the adjacent bhashya objection-response structure.
